details.text: Derivative: ضُحَا – ضُحَى refers to the time when the sun rises after sunrise and in conjunction to the sun it refers to the manifestation of its rays and light. Here it refers to the morning light that shines and radiates from the sun. ضُحَى when used to refer to light is exclusively only used in Arabic to describe the light that radiates from the sun.

Derivative: دَسَّا is in origin from دَسَّسَ – يُدَسِّسُ – تَدْسِيس which in origin means to conceal something or bury it in the dust or earth. Here it means to neglect, disregard and conceal its rightful place or right.
Derivative: خَابَ means to fail to attain something and entails disappointment. Its root letters are خ ي ب and contains within it the meanings of failure, disappointment and frustration.

Derivative: طَغْوَا is from طَغَى - يَطْغَى or طَغا – يَطْغُو which means to act rebelliously, in a manner exceeding and overstepping the proper limits, tyrannically and sinfully. طَغْوَا means طُغْيَان which refers to rebelliousness and exceeding the limits in terms of tyranny, oppression and the perpetration of sinful acts.
Derivative: أَشْقَا - أَشْقَى is the superlative (اِسمْ التَفْضِيل – Ism ut-Tafdeel) form of شَقِي and it means the most wretched, wicked or villainous.
Derivative: انبَعَثَ Means to voluntarily arise or rush to undertake an action or task.

فَقَالَ لَهُمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ نَاقَةَ اللَّهِ وَسُقْيَاهَا

- The word نَاقَةَ (Naaqata) which means she-camel is in the مَنْصُوب (Mansoob – Accusative) case although it is not preceded by a verb and this is because a verb is implied without being mentioned here. The verb that is missing can be “beware” or “leave be or alone” in the case where the نَاقَةَ (she-Camel) is being mentioned with a warning.
Derivative: عَقَرُوا from عَقَرَ which with a camel means to hamstring it to let it bleed out or to slaughter it.
Derivative: سَوَّا – سَوَّى means to make level, even out, bring proportion and balance. Here it means to flatten and can imply to restore balance.
Derivative: دَمْدَمَ means to crush and pound something until is flattened into the ground.

Derivative: ذَنبِ (Dhanb) means sin or crime. It originates from ذَنَب (Dhanab) which is the tail of an animal and was used as a symbol of shame and for being flawed amongst the Arabs. ذَنبِ (Dhanb) refers to that which is consequences are bad, it can refer to both minor and major acts and it relates to something that is a source of embarrassment or shame.
Derivative: عُقْبَا – عُقْبَى (‘Uqbaa) means consequence. It is the مُبَلَغَة (Mubaalaghah - hyperbolised) form of عَاقِبَة (‘Aaqibah – consequence or outcome).
Derivative: تَلَا comes from تَلا – يَتْلُو – تُلُو which means to follow behind or come right after something.

Derivative: جَلَّا from جَلَّى – يُجَلَّي – تَجْلِيَة which means to make manifest, to shine and be brilliant. In reference to the sun, it can be said to mean: “To make manifest its full brilliance and glory”.
Derivative: بَنَا is from بَنَى – يَبْنِي – بِنَاء and means to put things together in a way that they become inseparable i.e., to build and construct.

- The مَا (Maa) here can be ما مَوْصولَة with the meaning of مَنْ (Who), in which case it would refer to the One “who built and constructed the sky” to highlight how incredible He (Allah) is, or it can be مَا المَصْدَرِيَة in which case the meaning would be “how remarkable and incredible (is its construction - بَنَاهَا)”.
Derivative: طَحَا – يَطْحُو means to spread out.

- The مَا (Maa) here can be ما مَوْصولَة with the meaning of مَنْ (Who), in which case it would refer to the One “who spread the earth out” to highlight how incredible He (Allah) is, or it can be مَا المَصْدَرِيَة in which case the meaning would be “how remarkable and incredible (is its spreading out and vastness - طَحَاهَا)”.
Derivative: مَا -
Derivative: سَوَّا – سَوَّى comes from تَسْوِيَة which means to balance, level and even out things which are uneven. It is used here to mean to fashion and form.

- The oath here is being made by نَفْسٍ which is in the نَكِرَة (Nakirah - indefinite form) with Tanween, whilst all the oaths preceding it are in the مَعْرِفَة (Ma’rifah – definite form) with الْ. The use of the Nakirah here is a style for the purpose of magnifying it and to highlight it amongst the other oaths.

- The مَا (Maa) here can be ما مَوْصولَة with the meaning of مَنْ (Who), in which case it would refer to the One “who balanced, fashioned and proportioned it” to highlight how incredible He (Allah) is, or it can be مَا المَصْدَرِيَة in which case the meaning would be “how remarkable and incredible (is its balancing, proportioning and fashioning - سَوَّاهَا)”.

Derivative: أَلْهَمَ – إِلْهام means to something inside of or to inspire someone or something. It can mean to provide or implant the ability to understand and recognise something i.e., the نَفْس (Nafs) was given an understanding and recognition of its فُجُورَ (Fujoor) and of its تَقْوَى (Taqwa).

Derivative: تَقْوَى refers to the consciousness to protect and seek precaution. Its root وَقِيَ implies both protecting from something bad to stay away from it and also to protect yourself from the punishment or bad consequences that result from it. Taqwa is manifested in maintaining obedience to Allah and abiding by the Halal and Haram.

Derivative: فُجُورَ has origins in فَجَرَ – فَجْراً which means to cleave open and unleash. The فَاجِر (Faajir) is the one who rebels against Allah in the worst possibly way. فُجُورَ (Fujoor) refers to those acts which are clearly rebellious to Allah and is often associated with the most immoral and debaucherous acts.

فَأَلْهَمَهَا فُجُورَهَا وَتَقْوَاهَا

- The majority of the scholars of Tafsir say that فُجُورَ refers to evil (الشَّرّ – Ash-Sharr) and تَقْوَى refers to good (الخَيْر – Al-Khair).
Derivative: قَدْ is used in the Arabic language for two purposes. One is to illustrate a statement that is already the case or has already happened. And the other is to indicate to certainty.

Derivative: أَفْلَحَ means to succeed or be successful. أَفْلَحَ - إِفْلَاح relates to the success of the farmer (الفَلَّاح) when he reaps his harvest after all the hard work that he had put in. أَفْلَحَ therefore refers to a success that is dependent on efforts being made.
Derivative: زَكَّا – زَكَّى – يُزَكِّي – تَزْكِيَة means to cleanse or purify something.
